title: firensia hirsuta
side_effects: Firensia hirsuta can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while ingesting its leaves can lead to dizziness and headaches. In rare cases, misuse of the plant can result in anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Additionally, the plant's sap can cause skin burns and blisters, especially when handled without proper protective gear.
